segments.text: I will watch my waysso that I will not sin with my tongue; I will guard my mouth with a muzzleas long as the wicked are present.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
I was speechless and still; I remained silent, even from speaking good,
and my sorrow was stirred.

My heart grew hot within me; as I mused, the fire burned.
Then I spoke with my tongue:

Show me, O LORD, my end and the measure of my days. 
Let me know how fleeting my life is.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You, indeed, have made my days as handbreadths, and my lifetime as nothing before You. Truly each man at his bestexists as but a breath. Selah
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Surely every man goes about like a phantom; surely he bustles in vain; he heaps up richesnot knowing who will haul them away.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And now, O Lord, for what do I wait? My hope is in You.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Deliver me from all my transgressions; do not make me the reproach of fools.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
I have become mute; I do not open my mouth
because of what You have done.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Remove Your scourge from me; I am perishing by the force of Your hand.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
You discipline and correct a man for his iniquity, consuming like a moth what he holds dear; 
surely each man is but a vapor. Selah
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear to my cry for help; do not be deaf to my weeping. For I am a foreigner dwelling with You, 
a stranger like all my fathers.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Turn Your gaze away from me, that I may again be cheered
before I depart and am no more.
segments.speaker: Unknown
